title: Keckiella breviflora
taxonomy: Keckiella breviflora is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is part of the genus Keckiella, which belongs to the order Lamiales. The species Keckiella breviflora is a shrub native to the southwestern regions of North America. It is often found growing in areas with well-drained soil and full sun exposure. Within the taxonomic hierarchy, Keckiella breviflora is a species within the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Keckiella breviflora, also known as short-flowered keckiella or short-flowered penstemon, is a perennial plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Keckiella breviflora include iridoids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Keckiella breviflora is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
